context.text: Mount Kilimanjaro is a dormant volcano in Tanzania and is the highest mountain in Africa at 5,895 metres.

question: What was the first country to give women the right to vote?

context.title: Women's suffrage
context.text: Women's suffrage is the right of women to vote in elections.

context.title: New Zealand and women's suffrage
context.text: New Zealand was the first self-governing country to give women the right to vote, in 1893.

question: How many continents are there on Earth?

context.title: Continents
context.text: A continent is one of several large landmasses on Earth.

context.title: List of continents
context.text: Most models recognize seven continents: Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Australia (Oceania), Europe, North America, and South America.

question: What is the longest wall ever built?

context.title: Longest walls
context.text: Throughout history, many great walls have been constructed around the world.

context.text: The Great Wall of China is a series of walls and fortifications, totalling more than 21,196 km in length, making it the longest wall ever built.

question: What is the national animal of India?

context.title: National symbols of India
context.text: India has several national symbols representing its culture and heritage.

context.text: The Bengal tiger is the national animal of India and is found primarily in the Indian subcontinent.

question: What is the most widely spoken language in the world?

context.text: There are thousands of languages spoken around the world.

context.title: English language
context.text: English is considered the most widely spoken language in the world when including both native and non-native speakers.

question: In what century was the printing press invented?

context.title: Printing press
context.text: The printing press is a device for transferring text or images to paper.

context.text: Johannes Gutenberg invented the first movable type printing press around 1440 in the 15th century in Mainz, Germany.

question: What is the capital of Australia?

context.title: Australia

context.text: Canberra is the capital city of Australia. It is situated within the Australian Capital Territory.

question: What is the name of the force that keeps planets in orbit around the Sun?

context.title: Planetary orbits
context.text: Planets orbit the Sun in elliptical paths as described by Kepler's laws of planetary motion.

context.title: Gravity
context.text: Gravity is the fundamental force of attraction between masses. It is the force that keeps planets in their orbits around the Sun.

question: What is the tallest mountain in the world?

context.title: Highest mountains
context.text: Mountains are measured from sea level to their peak elevation.

context.title: Mount Everest
context.text: Mount Everest, located in the Himalayas on the border between Nepal and Tibet, is the world's highest mountain at 8,848.86 metres.

question: What river flows through London?

context.title: London

context.title: River Thames
context.text: The River Thames flows through London and is the longest river entirely in England.

question: Who painted the ceiling of the Sistine Chapel?

context.title: Sistine Chapel

context.text: The Sistine Chapel ceiling was painted by Michelangelo between 1508 and 1512 at the commission of Pope Julius II.

question: What is the currency of Japan?

question: What country has the most pyramids in the world?

context.title: Pyramids

context.text: Sudan has the most pyramids of any country in the world, with over 200 known pyramids, outnumbering those in Egypt.

context.text: The United States has several popular sports including baseball, American football, basketball, and ice hockey.

question: What is the largest desert in the world?

context.title: Deserts of the world
context.text: A desert is a landscape that receives very little precipitation.

context.text: The Antarctic Desert is the world's largest desert at 14.2 million km², covering the continent of Antarctica.

question: What is the most spoken language in Africa?

context.text: Africa has over 2000 languages making it the most linguistically diverse continent.

context.text: Swahili is a Bantu language and the most widely spoken language in Africa as a lingua franca, with over 200 million speakers.

question: What gas do plants absorb from the atmosphere during photosynthesis?

context.text: Photosynthesis is a process used by plants to convert light energy into chemical energy.

context.text: During photosynthesis, plants absorb carbon dioxide from the atmosphere and use it along with water to produce glucose and oxygen.

question: Which country is home to the fjords?

context.title: Fjords
context.text: A fjord is a long, narrow inlet with steep sides or cliffs created by glacial erosion.

question: What is the boiling point of water at sea level?

context.title: Water properties
context.text: Water has several important physical properties that make it essential for life.

context.title: Boiling point of water
context.text: At sea level (standard atmospheric pressure), water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit).

question: What sea does the Rhine River empty into?

context.title: Rhine River
context.text: The Rhine is a European river that begins in Switzerland and flows through Germany and the Netherlands.

context.text: The Rhine empties into the North Sea through a delta shared with the Meuse and Scheldt rivers in the Netherlands.

question: What is the most widely practiced religion in Indonesia?

context.text: Indonesia has the world's largest Muslim population. Islam is practiced by about 87% of the population, making it the country's predominant religion.

context.text: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System, with its name describing the galaxy's appearance from Earth.

question: What is the largest lake in Africa?

context.text: Lake Victoria is the largest lake in Africa and the largest tropical lake in the world, shared by Uganda, Kenya, and Tanzania.

context.text: The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris.

question: What is the longest river in Europe?

context.text: Europe has many significant rivers including the Danube, Rhine, and Volga.

context.text: The Volga is the longest river in Europe, flowing 3,531 km through Russia before emptying into the Caspian Sea.
